title: Why good leaders make you feel safe | Simon Sinek | TED

transcript.chinese: 有一位名叫 William Swenson 上尉的男子最近因其 2009 年 9 月 8 日的行为而被授予国会荣誉勋章。那天，一队美国和阿富汗军队正在穿越阿富汗部分地区，以帮助保护一群政府官员，一群阿富汗政府官员，他们将与当地一些村庄的长老会面。
transcript.english: There's a man by the name of Captain William Swenson who recently was awarded the congressional Medal of Honor for his actions on September 8, 2009.

transcript.chinese: 纵队遭到伏击，并被三面包围，斯文森上尉因冲入实弹营救伤员和救出死者而获得认可。
transcript.english: On that day, a column of American and Afghan troops were making their way through a part of Afghanistan to help protect a group of government officials, a group of Afghan government officials, who would be meeting with some local village elders.

transcript.chinese: 他救出的人之一是一名中士，他和一名战友正在前往一架医疗救援直升机。
transcript.english: The column came under ambush, and was surrounded on three sides, and amongst many other things, Captain Swenson was recognized for running into live fire to rescue the wounded and pull out the dead.

transcript.chinese: 这一天的非凡之处在于，纯属巧合，一名医疗后送医务人员的头盔上恰好装有 GoPro 相机，并用相机捕捉到了整个场景。
transcript.english: One of the people he rescued was a sergeant, and he and a comrade were making their way to a medevac helicopter.

transcript.chinese: 它显示斯文森上尉和他的战友带着这名颈部中弹的受伤士兵。
transcript.english: And what was remarkable about this day is, by sheer coincidence, one of the medevac medics happened to have a GoPro camera on his helmet and captured the whole scene on camera.

transcript.chinese: 他们把他放进直升机，然后你看到斯文森上尉弯下腰吻了他一下，然后他转身去营救更多人。
transcript.english: It shows Captain Swenson and his comrade bringing this wounded soldier who had received a gunshot to the neck.

transcript.chinese: 我看到这个，心想，哪里来的这样的人？
transcript.english: They put him in the helicopter, and then you see Captain Swenson bend over and give him a kiss before he turns around to rescue more.

transcript.chinese: 那是什么？
transcript.english: I saw this, and I thought to myself, where do people like that come from?

transcript.chinese: 当你想要这样做时，那是一种很深很深的情感。
transcript.english: What is that?

transcript.chinese: 那里有一种爱，我想知道为什么我没有这样的人一起工作？
transcript.english: That is some deep, deep emotion, when you would want to do that.

transcript.chinese: 你知道，在军队里，他们会把奖章授予那些愿意牺牲自己以使他人获益的人。
transcript.english: There's a love there, and I wanted to know why is it that I don't have people that I work with like that?

transcript.chinese: 在商业上，我们向那些愿意牺牲他人以便我们获得利益的人提供奖金。
transcript.english: You know, in the military, they give medals to people who are willing to sacrifice themselves so that others may gain.

transcript.chinese: 我们把它倒退了。
transcript.english: In business, we give bonuses to people who are willing to sacrifice others so that we may gain.

transcript.chinese: 正确的？
transcript.english: We have it backwards.

transcript.chinese: 于是我问自己，这样的人从哪里来？
transcript.english: Right?

transcript.chinese: 我最初的结论是他们只是更好的人。
transcript.english: So I asked myself, where do people like this come from?

transcript.chinese: 这就是他们被军队吸引的原因。
transcript.english: And my initial conclusion was that they're just better people.

transcript.chinese: 这些优秀的人被这种服务理念所吸​​引。
transcript.english: That's why they're attracted to the military.

transcript.chinese: 但这是完全错误的。
transcript.english: These better people are attracted to this concept of service.

transcript.chinese: 我学到的是，这就是环境，如果环境合适，我们每个人都有能力做出这些非凡的事情，更重要的是，其他人也有这种能力。
transcript.english: But that's completely wrong.

transcript.chinese: 我非常荣幸地见到了其中一些人，他们被我们称为英雄，他们冒着生命危险去拯救他人，我问他们：“你们为什么要这么做？
transcript.english: What I learned was that it's the environment, and if you get the environment right, every single one of us has the capacity to do these remarkable things, and more importantly, others have that capacity too.

transcript.chinese: 你们为什么这么做？”他们都说同样的话：“因为他们会为我做这件事。”正是这种深厚的信任与合作意识。
transcript.english: I've had the great honor of getting to meet some of these, who we would call heroes, who have put themselves and put their lives at risk to save others, and I asked them, "Why would you do it?

transcript.chinese: 因此，信任与合作在这里非常重要。
transcript.english: Why did you do it?" And they all say the same thing: "Because they would have done it for me." It's this deep sense of trust and cooperation.

transcript.chinese: 信任与合作概念的问题在于它们只是感觉，而不是指令。
transcript.english: So trust and cooperation are really important here.

transcript.chinese: 我不能简单地对你说：“相信我”，你就会这么做。
transcript.english: The problem with concepts of trust and cooperation is that they are feelings, they are not instructions.

transcript.chinese: 我不能简单地指示两个人合作，他们就会合作。
transcript.english: I can't simply say to you, "Trust me," and you will.

transcript.chinese: 它不是这样运作的。
transcript.english: I can't simply instruct two people  to cooperate, and they will.

transcript.chinese: 这是一种感觉。
transcript.english: It's not how it works.

transcript.chinese: 那么这种感觉从何而来呢？
transcript.english: It's a feeling.

transcript.chinese: 如果你回到五万年前的旧石器时代，回到智人的早期，我们会发现世界充满了危险，所有这些力量都非常非常努力地想要杀死我们。
transcript.english: So where does that feeling come from?

transcript.chinese: 没什么个人的。
transcript.english: If you go back 50,000 years to the Paleolithic era, to the early days of Homo sapiens, what we find is that the world was filled with danger, all of these forces working very, very hard to kill us.

transcript.chinese: 无论是天气、资源匮乏，还是剑齿虎，所有这些因素都会缩短我们的寿命。
transcript.english: Nothing personal.

transcript.chinese: 因此，我们进化成了社会性动物，我们在部落内部的一个我称之为安全圈的地方一起生活、一起工作，在那里我们感觉自己有归属感。
transcript.english: Whether it was the weather, lack of resources, maybe a saber-toothed tiger, all of these things working to reduce our lifespan.

transcript.chinese: 当我们在彼此之间感到安全时，自然的反应就是信任与合作。
transcript.english: And so we evolved into social animals, where we lived together and worked together in what I call a circle of safety, inside the tribe, where we felt like we belonged.

transcript.chinese: 这有其固有的好处。
transcript.english: And when we felt safe amongst our own, the natural reaction was trust and cooperation.

transcript.chinese: 这意味着我可以在晚上入睡，并相信我部落中的某个人会留意危险。
transcript.english: There are inherent benefits to this.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们不信任彼此，如果我不信任你，那就意味着你不会警惕危险。
transcript.english: It means I can fall asleep at night and trust that someone from within my tribe will watch for danger.

transcript.chinese: 糟糕的生存系统。
transcript.english: If we don't trust each other, if I don't trust you, that means you won't watch for danger.

transcript.chinese: 现代也是同样的情况。
transcript.english: Bad system of survival.

transcript.chinese: 世界充满了危险，那些事物试图挫败我们的生活或减少我们的成功，减少我们成功的机会。
transcript.english: The modern day is exactly the same thing.

transcript.chinese: 可能是经济的起伏、股市的不确定性。
transcript.english: The world is filled with danger, things that are trying to frustrate our lives or reduce our success, reduce our opportunity for success.

transcript.chinese: 它可能是一项新技术，让您的商业模式在一夜之间变得过时。
transcript.english: It could be the ups and downs in the economy, the uncertainty of the stock market.

transcript.chinese: 或者有时可能是你的竞争对手试图杀死你。
transcript.english: It could be a new technology that renders your business model obsolete overnight.

transcript.chinese: 有时它会试图让你破产，但至少会努力阻碍你的成长并窃取你的业务。
transcript.english: Or it could be your competition that is sometimes trying to kill you.

transcript.chinese: 我们无法控制这些力量。
transcript.english: It's sometimes trying to put you out of business, but at the very minimum is working hard to frustrate your growth and steal your business from you.

transcript.chinese: 这些是一个常数，并且不会消失。
transcript.english: We have no control over these forces.

transcript.chinese: 这些是一个常数，并且不会消失。
transcript.english: These are a constant, and they're not going away.

transcript.chinese: 唯一的变量是组织内部的条件，这就是领导力的重要性，因为是领导者定下基调。
transcript.english: The only variable are the conditions inside the organization, and that's where leadership matters, because it's the leader that sets the tone.

transcript.chinese: 当领导者选择将组织内部人员的安全和生命放在第一位，牺牲他们的舒适感并牺牲切实的成果，以便让人们留下来并感到安全和有归属感时，非凡的事情就会发生。
transcript.english: When a leader makes the choice to put the safety and lives of the people inside the organization first, to sacrifice their comforts and sacrifice the tangible results, so that the people remain and feel safe and feel like they belong, remarkable things happen.

transcript.chinese: 我在一次旅行中目睹了一次事件，一名乘客在叫号之前试图登机，我看到登机口工作人员对待这个人就像他触犯了法律，就像一个罪犯一样。
transcript.english: I was flying on a trip, and I was witness to an incident where a passenger attempted to board before their number was called, and I watched the gate agent treat this man like he had broken the law, like a criminal.

transcript.chinese: 他因试图过早登上一组而受到呵斥。
transcript.english: He was yelled at for attempting to board one group too soon.

transcript.chinese: 所以我说了一些话。
transcript.english: So I said something.

transcript.chinese: 我说：“你们为什么把我们当作牛一样对待？
transcript.english: I said, "Why do you have treat us like cattle?

transcript.chinese: 为什么你们不能把我们当作人来对待？”这正是她对我说的。
transcript.english: Why can't you treat us like human beings?" And this is exactly what she said to me.

transcript.chinese: 她说：“先生，如果我不遵守规则，我可能会遇到麻烦或丢掉工作。”她只是告诉我她没有安全感。
transcript.english: She said, "Sir, if I don't follow the rules, I could get in trouble or lose my job." All she was telling me is that she doesn't feel safe.

transcript.chinese: 她只是告诉我她不信任她的领导者。
transcript.english: All she was telling me is that she doesn't trust her leaders.

transcript.chinese: 我们喜欢乘坐西南航空的原因并不是因为他们一定会雇用更好的人才。
transcript.english: The reason we like flying Southwest Airlines is not because they necessarily hire better people.

transcript.chinese: 这是因为他们不惧怕他们的领袖。
transcript.english: It's because they don't fear their leaders.

transcript.chinese: 你看，如果条件不对，我们就被迫花费自己的时间和精力来保护自己免受彼此的伤害，这本质上会削弱组织。
transcript.english: You see, if the conditions are wrong, we are forced to expend our own time and energy to protect ourselves from each other, and that inherently weakens the organization.

transcript.chinese: 当我们在组织内部感到安全时，我们自然会结合自己的才能和优势，不懈地努力面对外部的危险并抓住机遇。
transcript.english: When we feel safe inside the organization, we will naturally combine our talents and our strengths and work tirelessly to face the dangers outside and seize the opportunities.

transcript.chinese: 对于什么是伟大的领导者，我能给出的最接近的比喻就是成为父母。
transcript.english: The closest analogy I can give to what a great leader is, is like being a parent.

transcript.chinese: 如果你思考什么是一个伟大的父母，你想要什么？
transcript.english: If you think about what being a great parent is, what do you want?

transcript.chinese: 是什么造就了一个伟大的父母？
transcript.english: What makes a great parent?

transcript.chinese: 我们希望为我们的孩子提供机会、教育，并在必要时对他们进行管教，以便他们能够成长并取得比我们自己更大的成就。
transcript.english: We want to give our child opportunities, education, discipline them when necessary, all so that they can grow up and achieve more than we could for ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 伟大的领导者想要完全相同的东西。
transcript.english: Great leaders want exactly the same thing.

transcript.chinese: 他们希望为员工提供机会、教育和必要时的纪律，建立他们的自信，给他们尝试和失败的机会，所有这些都是为了他们能够取得比我们自己想象的更多的成就。
transcript.english: They want to provide their people opportunity, education, discipline when necessary, build their self-confidence, give them the opportunity to try and fail, all so that they could achieve more than we could ever imagine for ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 查理·金 (Charlie Kim) 是纽约一家名为 Next Jump 的科技公司的首席执行官，他指出，如果你的家庭遇到困难，你会考虑解雇你的一个孩子吗？
transcript.english: Charlie Kim, who's the CEO of a company called Next Jump in New York City, a tech company, he makes the point that if you had hard times in your family, would you ever consider laying off one of your children?

transcript.chinese: 我们永远不会这样做。
transcript.english: We would never do it.

transcript.chinese: 那么我们为什么要考虑在组织内部裁员呢？
transcript.english: Then why do we consider laying off people inside our organization?

transcript.chinese: 查理实行终身雇佣政策。
transcript.english: Charlie implemented a policy of lifetime employment.

transcript.chinese: 如果您在 Next Jump 找到工作，您就不会因为绩效问题而被解雇。
transcript.english: If you get a job at Next Jump, you cannot get fired for performance issues.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，如果你遇到问题，他们会指导你并为你提供支持，就像我们对待一个碰巧从学校拿 C 回家的孩子一样。
transcript.english: In fact, if you have issues, they will coach you and they will give you support, just like we would with one of our children who happens to come home with a C from school.

transcript.chinese: 这是完全相反的。
transcript.english: It's the complete opposite.

transcript.chinese: 这就是为什么很多人对一些银行业首席执行官不成比例的工资和奖金结构抱有发自内心的仇恨和愤怒。
transcript.english: This is the reason so many people have such a visceral hatred, anger, at some of these banking CEOs with their disproportionate salaries and bonus structures.

transcript.chinese: 这不是数字。
transcript.english: It's not the numbers.

transcript.chinese: 他们违反了领导力的定义。
transcript.english: It's that they have violated the very definition of leadership.

transcript.chinese: 他们违反了这一根深蒂固的社会契约。
transcript.english: They have violated this deep-seated social contract.

transcript.chinese: 我们知道，他们允许牺牲自己的人民，以便保护自己的利益，或者更糟的是，他们牺牲人民来保护自己的利益。
transcript.english: We know that they allowed their people to be sacrificed so they could protect their own interests, or worse, they sacrificed their people to protect their own interests.

transcript.chinese: 让我们感到如此冒犯的是这个，而不是数字。
transcript.english: This is what so offends us, not the numbers.

transcript.chinese: 如果我们向甘地提供 1.5 亿美元奖金，有人会生气吗？
transcript.english: Would anybody be offended if we gave a $150 million bonus to Gandhi?

transcript.chinese: 给特蕾莎修女2.5亿美元的奖金怎么样？
transcript.english: How about a $250 million bonus to Mother Teresa?

transcript.chinese: 我们对此有什么问题吗？
transcript.english: Do we have an issue with that?

transcript.chinese: 一点也没有。
transcript.english: None at all.

transcript.chinese: 一点也没有。
transcript.english: None at all.

transcript.chinese: 伟大的领导人绝不会为了拯救人数而牺牲人民。
transcript.english: Great leaders would never sacrifice the people to save the numbers.

transcript.chinese: 他们宁愿牺牲人数来拯救人民。
transcript.english: They would sooner sacrifice the numbers to save the people.

transcript.chinese: 鲍勃·查普曼 (Bob Chapman) 在中西部经营着一家名为 Barry-Wehmiller 的大型制造公司，2008 年，他受到经济衰退的严重打击，一夜之间损失了 30% 的订单。
transcript.english: Bob Chapman, who runs a large manufacturing company in the Midwest called Barry-Wehmiller, in 2008 was hit very hard by the recession, and they lost 30 percent of their orders overnight.

transcript.chinese: 现在在一家大型制造公司，这是一件大事，他们再也负担不起劳动力了。
transcript.english: Now in a large manufacturing company, this is a big deal, and they could no longer afford their labor pool.

transcript.chinese: 他们需要节省 1000 万美元，因此，像今天的许多公司一样，董事会聚在一起讨论裁员问题。
transcript.english: They needed to save 10 million dollars, so, like so many companies today, the board got together and discussed layoffs.

transcript.chinese: 鲍勃拒绝了。
transcript.english: And Bob refused.

transcript.chinese: 你看，鲍勃不相信人数。
transcript.english: You see, Bob doesn't believe in head counts.

transcript.chinese: 鲍勃相信心脏计数，而简单地减少心脏计数要困难得多。
transcript.english: Bob believes in heart counts, and it's much more difficult to simply reduce the heart count.

transcript.chinese: 所以他们想出了一个休假计划。
transcript.english: And so they came up with a furlough program.

transcript.chinese: 从秘书到首席执行官，每位员工都必须休四个星期的无薪假期。
transcript.english: Every employee, from secretary to CEO, was required to take four weeks of unpaid vacation.

transcript.chinese: 他们可以随时服用，而且不必连续服用。
transcript.english: They could take it any time they wanted, and they did not have to take it consecutively.

transcript.chinese: 但鲍勃宣布该计划的方式才是最重要的。
transcript.english: But it was how Bob announced the program that mattered so much.

transcript.chinese: 他说，我们大家都受一点苦比我们任何一个人受很多苦要好，士气大振。
transcript.english: He said, it's better that we should all suffer a little than any of us should have to suffer a lot, and morale went up.

transcript.chinese: 他们节省了 2000 万美元，最重要的是，正如预期的那样，当人们感到安全并受到组织领导层的保护时，自然的反应是信任和合作。
transcript.english: They saved 20 million dollars, and most importantly, as would be expected, when the people feel safe and protected by the leadership in the organization, the natural reaction is to trust and cooperate.

transcript.chinese: 出乎意料的是，人们自然而然地开始相互交易。
transcript.english: And quite spontaneously, nobody expected, people started trading with each other.

transcript.chinese: 那些负担能力较高的人会与负担能力较差的人进行交易。
transcript.english: Those who could afford it more would trade with those who could afford it less.

transcript.chinese: 人们需要五周的时间，而其他人只需三周的时间。
transcript.english: People would take five weeks so that somebody else only had to take three.

transcript.chinese: 领导力是一种选择。
transcript.english: Leadership is a choice.

transcript.chinese: 这不是一个等级。
transcript.english: It is not a rank.

transcript.chinese: 我认识许多处于组织最高层的人，他们绝对不是领导者。
transcript.english: I know many people at the seniormost levels of organizations who are absolutely not leaders.

transcript.chinese: 他们是权威，我们按照他们说的做，因为他们对我们有权威，但我们不会跟随他们。
transcript.english: They are authorities, and we do what they say because they have authority over us, but we would not follow them.

transcript.chinese: 我认识很多处于组织底层的人，他们没有权力，但他们是绝对的领导者，这是因为他们选择照顾他们左边的人，他们选择照顾他们右边的人。
transcript.english: And I know many people who are at the bottoms of organizations who have no authority and they are absolutely leaders, and this is because they have chosen to look after the person to the left of them, and they have chosen to look after the person to the right of them.

transcript.chinese: 这就是领导者。
transcript.english: This is what a leader is.

transcript.chinese: 我听说过一些海军陆战队员在战区的故事，按照海军陆战队的习俗，军官最后吃东西，他让他的部下先吃，当他们吃完时，他就没有食物了。
transcript.english: I heard a story of some Marines who were out in theater, and as is the Marine custom, the officer ate last, and he let his men eat first, and when they were done, there was no food left for him.

transcript.chinese: 当他们回到田野时，他的手下给他带来了一些食物，以便他可以吃，因为事情就是这样。
transcript.english: And when they went back out in the field, his men brought him some of their food so that he may eat, because that's what happens.

transcript.chinese: 我们称他们为领导者，因为他们先行一步。
transcript.english: We call them leaders because they go first.

transcript.chinese: 我们称他们为领导者，因为他们先于其他人承担风险。
transcript.english: We call them leaders because they take the risk before anybody else does.

transcript.chinese: 我们称他们为领导者，因为他们会选择牺牲，以便他们的人民能够安全和受到保护，这样他们的人民就能获益，而当我们这样做时，自然的反应是我们的人民将为我们牺牲。
transcript.english: We call them leaders because they will choose to sacrifice so that their people may be safe and protected and so their people may gain, and when we do, the natural response is that our people will sacrifice for us.

transcript.chinese: 他们会为我们奉献自己的血汗和泪水，以确保他们的领导人的愿景成为现实，当我们问他们：“你们为什么要这样做？
transcript.english: They will give us their blood and sweat and tears to see that their leader's vision comes to life, and when we ask them, "Why would you do that?

transcript.chinese: 为什么你们要为那个人付出你们的血汗和泪水？”他们都说同样的话：“因为他们会为我做这件事。”这难道不是我们都想在其中工作的组织吗？
transcript.english: Why would you give your blood and sweat and tears for that person?" they all say the same thing: "Because they would have done it for me." And isn't that the organization we would all like to work in?

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: Thank you very much.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。 （鼓掌）谢谢。 （掌声）
transcript.english: Thank you. (Applause) Thank you. (Applause)
